title: Jurinea dolomiaea
intro: Jurinea dolomiaea, also known as Dolomiaea, is a perennial plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Jurinea dolomiaea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Jurinea dolomiaea is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical ointments and creams, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Jurinea dolomiaea is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Jurinea. The genus Jurinea is part of the tribe Cardueae, and the family Asteraceae is part of the clade Rosids. The classification of Jurinea dolomiaea reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants. At the most basic level, Jurinea dolomiaea is a member of the kingdom Plantae.
